agent_invocations_validation.violation_message:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Expected: 3 agent invocations
Actual: 2 completed invocations

Missing phases:
  - Phase 3 (Implementation)

Cannot generate completion report until all agents are invoked.
Review the AGENT INVOCATIONS LOG above to see which phases were skipped.

AGENT INVOCATIONS LOG:
✅ Phase 4 (Phase 4): test-orchestrator (completed in ?s)
✅ Phase 5 (Phase 5): code-reviewer (completed in ?s)

TASK WILL BE MOVED TO BLOCKED STATE
Reason: Protocol violation - required agents not invoked
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

completion_promises.criterion_text: A search returns a single assembled context block (not a raw list).
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented assemble_context() function that returns AssemblyResult containing a single context_block string. Tests verify empty and populated blocks are returned as single strings.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The assembled block never exceeds the token budget, measured with tiktoken cl100k_base. (AC-1)
completion_promises.evidence: Token measurement uses tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base') to measure the actual assembled block. The incremental assembly loop checks candidate_tokens <= token_budget before accepting each item. Test test_assembled_block_never_exceeds_budget verifies this.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A block whose assembled size is exactly the budget (e.g. 2000) is returned in full and measures exactly the budget. (boundary: just-inside)
completion_promises.evidence: Assembly logic uses <= comparison, so content exactly at budget is accepted. Test test_exactly_at_budget_returns_full verifies boundary condition.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Content that would push the block past the budget is dropped rather than overflowing; the lowest-ranked memories are the ones omitted. (boundary: 2100 → drop lowest)
completion_promises.evidence: When candidate_tokens > token_budget, the loop breaks, leaving remaining (lower-ranked) items unprocessed. Test test_over_budget_drops_lowest_ranked verifies lowest-ranked items are dropped.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A zero token budget returns an empty context block (not an error); coverage reports nothing filled.
completion_promises.evidence: Special case handling at function start: if token_budget == 0, return AssemblyResult with empty string, coverage_score=0.0, and tokens_used=0. Test test_zero_budget_returns_empty_block verifies this.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A single memory larger than the entire budget is omitted whole, not truncated mid-content; an empty block is returned and coverage reports the budget could not be filled. (ASSUM-009, low confidence — carried as open)
completion_promises.evidence: Assembly logic never truncates content. If the first item exceeds budget, it's rejected in the first iteration and the loop terminates, returning empty block. Test test_single_memory_larger_than_budget_omitted verifies this.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the budget forces a cut, the higher-ranked memory is kept and the lower-ranked one omitted (a highly relevant warning beats a barely relevant overview).
completion_promises.evidence: Assembly processes items in order from ranked_results (already sorted by relevance descending). Loop accepts items in order until budget exhausted. Test test_higher_ranked_kept_over_lower verifies high-scored items are kept.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The result reports a coverage score: the fraction (0.0–1.0) of the budget filled, plus the set of payload types that contributed to the block.
completion_promises.evidence: AssemblyResult dataclass includes coverage_score (tokens_used / token_budget) and contributing_types set extracted from natural_key prefixes. Tests test_coverage_score_reported and test_contributing_types_extracted verify this.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A search that cannot fill the budget reports partial coverage honestly (not padded).
completion_promises.evidence: Coverage score is calculated as actual tokens_used / token_budget. No padding or artificial inflation. Test test_partial_coverage_reported_honestly verifies coverage < 1.0 when budget not filled.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Repeated searches over an unchanged corpus return an identical assembled block (assembly is deterministic given identical ranked input).
completion_promises.evidence: Assembly logic is purely deterministic: processes items in order, uses fixed encoding, no random or time-dependent behavior. Test test_deterministic_assembly verifies repeated calls return identical results.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'ruff check src/fleet_memory/retrieval/' and received 'All checks passed!' confirmation. Both implementation and test files follow project style (type hints, docstrings, PEP 8).
